purpose: PreToolUse hook on Bash. Defense-in-depth companion to enforce-tracker-root-writes.sh — blocks bash commands that would write dashboard files (initialization.json, logs.json, master_state.json, metrics.json, progress/*.json) to {project_root}/dashboards/ instead of {tracker_root}/dashboards/. Reads project_root from .synapse/project.json, computes tracker_root from script location. If tracker_root == project_root (Synapse targeting itself), no conflict possible — allow. Otherwise blocks any command containing `{project_root}/dashboards/` and tells the agent the correct path.
gotchas: Pattern matches with shell case globs — won't catch quoted paths inside JSON-escaped strings
Only inspects literal command text — variables like `cd $DIR/dashboards` may slip through if $DIR holds project_root
Fail-open if .synapse/project.json missing or project_root unset — supports unconfigured Synapse
patterns: Glob-based command pattern matching for write redirects targeting tracker file shapes
Compares tracker_root vs project_root resolved via .synapse/project.json
